text:
ra Span (of time)
Yama ofJupiter = Yemakandaka Span (of time)
Yama of Venus= Yamasukra Span (of time)
Y ama of Saturn = Gulika Span (of time)
Note:- Span = span of time (this is a word I am adding to
avoid later confusion with Kshenika grahas with the same
name)
Note that here the words Kala, Paridhi, Dhooma,
Ardhaprahara, Yemakandaka, Yamasukra and Gulika
represent a span of time covering I hour 30 min approx.
Thus Kala span is the name ofYama number 1, 7, 6, 5, 4,
3, 2 in day time starting from Sunday to Saturday respectively.
Means, on Sunday first Yama is known as Kala span (Yama
of sun), on Monday the 7th yama is known as Kala span (yama
of sun), on Wednesday 6th yama is known as Kala span (yama
of sun) and so on. On night Kala would be 1,7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2
yamas starting from Thursday to Wednesday respectively.
If we are considering a single day, for example Sunday,
the first Y ama is Kala span (Y ama of Sun), second yama is
Paridhi span (yama of Moon) and so on.
